blocks:
Sūrat Saba

Meccan, its number of verses is fifty-five verses. The Shami count is four, and in the remaining (readings, the count is fifty-five). The point of difference is the verse عَنْ يَمِينٍ وَشِمالٍ بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (1) All praise belongs to Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. (It is) a word of blessing from Allah, so all praise belongs to Him in the world (الدنيا) وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ ** and all praise belongs to Him in the Hereafter. because its blessings are also entirely from Allah وَهُوَ الْحَكِيمُ ** and He is the All-wise, Who perfected the affair of the two abodes الْخَبِيرُ ** the All-aware. of the inner realities (بواطن) of things.

(٢) يَعْلَمُ ما يَلِجُ enters into the earth, such as rain, or treasure, or a dead [body] وَما يَخْرُجُ مِنْها such as water, or metal/ore (فلزّ), or vegetation, or animal وَما يَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّماءِ such as rain, or an angel (ملك), or provision (رزق) وَما يَعْرُجُ فِيها such as a deed (عمل) or an angel (ملك) وَهُوَ الرَّحِيمُ الْغَفُورُ for those who fall short (المقصّرين) in thanking His blessings.

(2) He knows what penetrates the earth, and what emerges from it, and what descends from the sky, and what ascends to it. And He is the All-merciful, the All-forgiving.

(٣) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لا تَأْتِينَا السَّاعَةُ ** And those who are faithless say, 'The Hour will not overtake us.' This is a denial of its coming, or a scornful impatience regarding the promise of it. قُلْ بَلى وَرَبِّي ** Say, 'Yes, by my Lord, This is a refutation of their speech and an affirmation of what He has uttered. لَتَأْتِيَنَّكُمْ عالِمِ الْغَيْبِ ** it will surely overtake you, the Knower of the Unseen.' This is a repetition of its necessity, confirmed by the oath, and established by describing the One sworn by with attributes that affirm its possibility and negate its improbability. And it was recited (علّام) and with the raf' (nominative case). لا يَعْزُبُ عَنْهُ مِثْقالُ ذَرَّةٍ فِي السَّماواتِ وَلا فِي الْأَرْضِ ** Not [even] the weight of an atom escapes His notice in the heavens or on the earth, And it was recited (لا يعزب) with the kasrah (vowel i). وَلا أَصْغَرُ مِنْ ذلِكَ وَلا أَكْبَرُ إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ مُبِينٍ ** nor anything smaller than that, nor anything greater, but it is in a manifest Book. Their raf' (nominative case) is due to them being the subject (الابتداء), and the sentence confirms the negation of escaping notice (نفي العزوب). And it was recited with the fath (accusative case) based on the negation of the genus (نفي الجنس).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: The first thing Allah created was the Pen (القلم). He said to it: "Write." So it wrote what was and what will be until the Day of Resurrection. لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ ** that He may reward those who have faith and do righteous deeds. This is the reason (علّة) for its coming and an explanation of what it necessitates. أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ ** For such there is forgiveness and a noble provision. There is no toil in it, nor any obligation (منّ) placed upon him (the recipient).

(٥) وَالَّذِينَ سَعَوْا فِي آياتِنا ** by invalidating them and making people lose interest in them مُعاجِزِينَ ** racing so that they might escape Us. And it was read (وقرئ) as معجزين (rendering incapable), meaning: those who discourage from faith whoever desires it. أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ مِنْ رِجْزٍ ** from the worst of punishment أَلِيمٌ ** afflicting (مؤلم). And it was read (وقرئ) with the nominative case (بالرّفع).

(٦) وَيَرَى الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا (١) الْعِلْمَ الَّذِي أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ هُوَ الْحَقَ (And those who have been given knowledge see that what has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth) Al-Qummi said: He (هو) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), who affirmed the truth of the Messenger of Allah regarding what Allah sent down to him. And it was read with the rafa' (رفع) (nominative case) of al-Haqq (الحقّ). وَيَهْدِي إِلى صِراطِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَمِيدِ ** (and guides to the path of the All-mighty, the All-laudable.) which is al-Tawhid (التوحيد) and donning the garment of piety (التقوى).

(٧) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا Some of them said to others: هَلْ نَدُلُّكُمْ عَلى رَجُلٍ They mean the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله يُنَبِّئُكُمْ He tells you of the most astonishing of astonishing things إِذا مُزِّقْتُمْ كُلَّ مُمَزَّقٍ إِنَّكُمْ لَفِي خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ ** (7) AND THE FAITHLESS SAY, ‘SHALL WE SHOW YOU A MAN WHO WILL INFORM YOU THAT WHEN YOU ARE TOTALLY TORN TO PIECES, YOU WILL BE IN A NEW CREATION?’ Indeed, you will be brought forth as a new creation after your bodies have been completely torn apart and scattered, such that they become dust (تراباً).

(٨) أَفْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً أَمْ بِهِ جِنَّةٌ ** (8) Has he fabricated a lie against Allah, or is there a madness in him? madness (جنون) that makes him imagine that and casts it upon his tongue. ** بَلِ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ فِي الْعَذابِ وَالضَّلالِ الْبَعِيدِ ** Rather those who do not believe in the Hereafter are in the punishment and the far error. A refutation (ردّ) from Allah against their repetition (of these accusations). (٩) أَفَلَمْ يَرَوْا إِلى ما بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَما خَلْفَهُمْ ** (9) Have they not regarded what is before them and what is behind them what encompasses their sides (جوانبهم) of the heaven and the earth, which indicates the perfection of Allah's power, and that they are within His dominion (سلطانه), and His power runs its course upon them. ** إِنْ نَشَأْ نَخْسِفْ بِهِمُ الْأَرْضَ أَوْ نُسْقِطْ عَلَيْهِمْ كِسَفاً مِنَ السَّماءِ ** If We wish, We will make the earth swallow them, or let fall upon them fragments from the sky. due to their denial of the signs (الآيات) after the clear proofs (البيّنات) have appeared. وقرئ بالياء في ثلاثتهنّ وكسفاً بتحريك السّين ** إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ ** And it was read (وقرئ) with the yā' (بالياء) in all three (verbs) and kisafan (وكسفاً) with the vocalization (تحريك) of the sīn (السّين). There is indeed in that The contemplation (النظر) and reflection (الفكر) upon them (heaven and earth) and what they indicate ** لَآيَةً ** a sign (لَدَلالَة) لِكُلِّ عَبْدٍ مُنِيبٍ for every penitent servant. returning (راجع) to his Lord, for he is one who reflects much (كثير التأمّل) upon His command (أمره).

(١٠) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا داوُدَ مِنَّا فَضْلاً يا جِبالُ أَوِّبِي ** (10) And certainly We gave David a grace from Us: 'O mountains, respond [with praise] ارجعي مَعَهُ التسبيح. meaning: return/repeat with him the glorification. القمّيّ أي سبّحي لله وَالطَّيْرَ أي ارجعي أيضاً أو أنت والطَّيْرَ *وقرئ* بالرّفع وَأَلَنَّا لَهُ الْحَدِيدَ جعلناه في يده كالشمع يصرفه كيف يشاء من غير احماء وطرق. Al-Qummi said: meaning, glorify Allah. and the birds (34:10) meaning: return/repeat also, or [it means] you and the birds. And it was read with raf'. and We softened the iron for him (34:10) We made it in his hand like wax, He could shape it however He willed without heating or hammering. القمّيّ قال : كان داود إذا مرّ بالبراري يقرأ الزبور تسبّح الجبال والطير معه والوحوش والان الله له الحديد مثل الشمع حتّى كان يتّخذ منه ما أحبّ وقال أعطى داود وسليمان ما لم يعط أحداً من أنبياء الله من الآيات علّمهما منطق الطير وألان لهما الحديد والصفر من غير نار وجعلت الجبال يسبحن مع داود. Al-Qummi said: David, when He passed through the deserts (البراري), would recite the Psalms (الزبور), and the mountains, the birds, and the wild beasts (الوحوش) would glorify [Allah] with Him. And Allah softened the iron for Him like wax, so that He would fashion from it whatever He loved. He said: David and Solomon were given what no other prophet of Allah was given in terms of signs (الآيات): He taught them the speech of the birds (منطق الطير), and He softened the iron and the copper (الصفر) for them without fire, and the mountains were made to glorify [Allah] with David.

footnote (١) يعني القرآن **هُوَ الْحَقَ **أي يعلمونه الحق لأنّهم يتدبرونه ويتفكرون فيه فيعلمون بالنظر والاستدلال انه ليس من قبل البشر (1) meaning the Quran it is the truth (34:6) meaning they know it is the truth because they ponder over it and reflect upon it, so they know through observation and inference (الاستدلال) that it is not from mankind.

(١١) أَنِ اعْمَلْ سابِغاتٍ ** (11) [Saying:] 'Make full coats of mail, wide coats of mail (دروعاً واسعات). وَقَدِّرْ فِي السَّرْدِ ** and maintain proportion in the links [of the chain mail].' in its weaving (نسجها) such that its rings (حلقها) are proportionate, or in its rivets (مساميرها) regarding fineness (الدقّة) and thickness (الغلظ), so that they neither become stuck (تغلق) nor tear (تحرق). In Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: "The ring (الحلقة) after the ring (الحلقة)." And al-Qummi said: "The rivets (المسامير) that are in the ring (الحلقة)." وَاعْمَلُوا صالِحاً إِنِّي بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ. And act righteously; indeed, I watch what you do.'

(١٢) وَلِسُلَيْمانَ الرِّيحَ ** (12) And for Solomon (We subjected) the wind: And We subjected the wind to him. It was read (وقرئ) with the nominative case (بالرفع): غُدُوُّها شَهْرٌ وَرَواحُها شَهْرٌ (its morning course was a month’s journey, and its evening course was a month’s journey). Its movement in the morning was a month's journey, and in the evening likewise. Al-Qummi said: The wind used to carry Solomon’s throne, traveling with it in the morning a month’s journey, and in the evening a month’s journey. وَأَسَلْنا لَهُ عَيْنَ الْقِطْرِ ** and We made the fountain of molten copper flow for him. Al-Qummi said: (الصفر) (copper). And it was said: He made molten copper flow for him from its mine, so it gushed forth from it like water gushing from a spring (الينبوع). For this reason, He named it a fountain (عيناً). And that was in Yemen (باليمن). وَمِنَ الْجِنِّ مَنْ يَعْمَلُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِ وَمَنْ يَزِغْ مِنْهُمْ عَنْ أَمْرِنا ** And among the jinn were those who worked before him by the leave of his Lord, and whoever of them deviated from Our command, meaning: whoever among them turns away (يعدل) from what We commanded him concerning obedience to Solomon. نُذِقْهُ مِنْ عَذابِ السَّعِيرِ ** We shall make him taste the punishment of the Blaze. It was said: the punishment of the Hereafter (عذاب الآخرة), and it was said: the punishment of the world (عذاب الدنيا).

(١٣) يَعْمَلُونَ لَهُ ما يَشاءُ مِنْ مَحارِيبَ ** (They made for him whatever he wished of shrines) fortified palaces and noble dwellings. They were named by this (name) because they are defended (يُذَبُّ عَنْهَا) and fought over them (يُحَارَبُ عَلَيْهَا). وَتَماثِيلَ ** (and statues) and images. In al-Kafi and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "By Allah, they were not statues of men and women, but rather they were trees and what resembles them." وَجِفانٍ ** (and bowls) platters كَالْجَوابِ ** (like cisterns) like large basins, the plural of jābiyah (جَابِيَة), derived from al-jibāyah (الجِبَايَة). وَقُدُورٍ راسِياتٍ ** (and cauldrons fixed [in the ground]) fixed upon the hearth-stones (الأَثَافِي), they are not taken down from them due to their immense size. اعْمَلُوا آلَ داوُدَ شُكْراً وَقَلِيلٌ مِنْ عِبادِيَ الشَّكُورُ ** (‘O family of David, act thankfully! But few of My servants are grateful.’) (The Shakūr - the grateful one) is he who is dedicated to performing gratitude with his heart, his tongue, and his limbs most of the time. Yet, despite that, he does not fulfill His right, because His granting him success (تَوْفِيقَهُ) for gratitude is a blessing which necessitates another act of gratitude without end. And for that reason, it was said: الشَّكُورُ ** (The grateful one) is he who sees his inability (عَجْزَهُ) to perform gratitude.

(١٤) فَلَمَّا قَضَيْنا عَلَيْهِ الْمَوْتَ (14) When We decreed death for him meaning, upon Sulayman (peace be upon him). ما دَلَّهُمْ عَلى مَوْتِهِ إِلَّا دَابَّةُ الْأَرْضِ nothing apprised them of his death except the earth worm meaning, the termite (الأرضة). The action (فعلها) of the earth (الأرض) was attributed to it. تَأْكُلُ مِنْسَأَتَهُ eating away his staff (مِنْسَأَتَهُ), which is His stick (عصاه), derived from (نساه) when He drove him away. فَلَمَّا خَرَّ تَبَيَّنَتِ الْجِنُّ أَنْ لَوْ كانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ الْغَيْبَ ما لَبِثُوا فِي الْعَذابِ الْمُهِينِ So when he fell down, the jinn realized that had they known the unseen, they would not have remained in the humiliating torment.

In al-Majma': And in the variant readings (الشواذ): the humans realized (تَبَيَّنَتِ الانس). Then he attributed it to al-Sajjad and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both), and its mention will follow.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to Sulayman ibn Dawud (peace be upon him) that the sign (آية) of His death would be a tree that emerges from Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem), called al-Kharrūbah (الخرنوبة). He said: One day Sulayman looked and behold, the Kharrūbah tree had sprouted from Bayt al-Maqdis. He asked it: "What is your name?" It replied: "al-Kharrūbah." He said: Sulayman turned away, heading towards His prayer niche (محرابه), and stood there leaning upon His staff. His soul was seized at that very hour. He said: The jinn and humans began serving Him and carrying out His commands as they used to, while they thought He was alive and had not died. They would go out in the morning and return in the evening, and He remained standing firm, until the termite (الأرضة) crept up His staff and ate away His staff (مِنْسَأَتَهُ), so it broke, and Sulayman fell to the ground. Do you not hear His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: So when he fell down, the jinn realized... the Ayah.

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: Sulayman ibn Dawud (peace be upon him) commanded the jinn, and they built for Him a dome (قبّة) of glass (قوارير). While He was leaning upon His staff inside the dome, watching how the jinn worked and they watching Him, He happened to glance aside, and behold, there was a man with Him in the dome. He was startled by him and asked him: "Who are you?" He replied: "I am the one who does not accept bribes (الرشا) and does not fear kings. I am the Angel of Death (ملك الموت)." So He seized His soul while He was leaning upon His staff in the dome, and the jinn were watching Him. He said: They remained working (يَدْابُونَ) for Him for a year, until Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent the termite (الأرضة), which ate away مِنْسَأَتَهُ his staff, which is the stick (العصا). فَلَمَّا خَرَّ تَبَيَّنَتِ الْجِنُ So when he fell down, the jinn realized... the Ayah. He (peace be upon him) said: The jinn are grateful to the termite (الأرضة) for what it did to Sulayman's staff, so you scarcely see it in any place except that it has water and clay (ماء وطين) nearby.

And al-Qummi said: When Allah revealed to Sulayman that He was going to die, He commanded the devils (الشياطين) to build Him a house of glass (قوارير). They placed it in the deep sea (لجّة البحر), and Sulayman entered it and leaned upon His staff. He was reciting the Psalms (الزّبور), and the devils were around Him, looking at Him, not daring to leave. While He was in that state, He happened to glance aside—then he mentioned a narration similar to the previous one—then he said: فَلَمَّا خَرَّ So when he fell down upon His face, the humans realized (تَبَيَّنَتِ الانس) that الْجِنُّ أَنْ لَوْ كانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ الْغَيْبَ ما لَبِثُوا فِي الْعَذابِ الْمُهِينِ the jinn realized that had they known the unseen, they would not have remained in the humiliating torment. This is how this Ayah was revealed. This is because the humans used to say that the jinn knew the unseen. So when Sulayman (peace be upon him) fell upon His face, they knew that if the jinn had known the unseen, they would not have worked for Sulayman (peace be upon him) for a year while He was dead, imagining Him to be alive.

In al-Uyun and al-Ilal, narrated from al-Rida, from His father, from His father (peace be upon them all): Sulayman ibn Dawud (peace be upon him) said one day to His companions: "Indeed, Allah the Exalted granted لِي مُلْكاً لا يَنْبَغِي لِأَحَدٍ مِنْ بَعْدِي me a kingdom that shall not belong to anyone after me. He subjugated the wind, the jinn, humans, birds, and wild beasts for me, and taught me the speech of the birds, and gave me of everything. Yet, with all the kingdom I have been given, I have not achieved a single day's joy from morning till night. I would like to enter My palace tomorrow, ascend to its highest point, and look upon My dominions, and do not permit anyone to approach Me, lest something occurs that spoils My day." They said: "Yes." When the next day came, He took His staff in His hand and ascended to the highest point of His palace. He stood leaning upon His staff, looking at His dominions, joyful at what He had been given, and delighted by what He had been granted. Suddenly, He saw a young man, handsome in face and clothing, emerge before Him from one of the corners of His palace. When Sulayman (peace be upon him) saw him, He asked him: "Who allowed you into this palace? I intended to be alone here today. By whose permission did you enter?" The young man replied: "The Lord (ربّه) of this palace admitted me, and by His permission I entered." Sulayman said: "His Lord has a greater right to it than I do. Who are you?" He replied: "I am the Angel of Death (ملك الموت)." Sulayman asked: "And for what purpose have you come?" He replied: "I have come to seize your soul." Sulayman said: "Proceed with what you have been commanded. This is the day of My joy, for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, refused that I should have joy short of meeting Him." So the Angel of Death seized His soul while He was leaning upon His staff. Sulayman remained leaning upon His staff, dead, for as long as Allah willed. The people were looking at Him, believing Him to be alive, and they were tested (افتتنوا) and differed concerning Him. Some of them said: "Sulayman (peace be upon him) has remained leaning upon His staff for these many days, without tiring, sleeping, eating, or drinking. He must be our Lord, Whom we must worship." Others said: "Sulayman is a sorcerer (ساحر), and He is showing us that He is standing leaning upon His staff, enchanting our eyes, but He is not truly so." The believers said: "Sulayman is the servant of Allah and His Prophet. Allah manages His affair as He wills." When they differed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent the termite (الأرضة), and it crept into His staff. When it ate away its core, the staff broke, and Sulayman fell from His palace upon His face. The jinn thanked the termite for its deed. For this reason, the termite is not found in any place except that it has water and clay (ماء وطين) nearby. That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَلَمَّا قَضَيْنا عَلَيْهِ الْمَوْتَ ما دَلَّهُمْ عَلى مَوْتِهِ إِلَّا دَابَّةُ الْأَرْضِ تَأْكُلُ مِنْسَأَتَهُ When We decreed death for him, nothing apprised them of his death except the earth worm, eating away his staff—meaning His staff. Al-Qummi: So when he fell down, the jinn realized that had they known... the Ayah.

Then al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: By Allah, this Ayah was not revealed this way. Rather, it was revealed: So when he fell down, the humans realized (تَبَيَّنَتِ الانس) that the jinn realized that had they known the unseen, they would not have remained in the humiliating torment.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked how the devils (الشياطين) ascended to the heaven (السماء) when they are like humans in creation and density (الكثافة), and they used to build for Sulayman ibn Dawud (peace be upon him) structures that the children of Adam are incapable of building. He said: "They were made dense (غلظوا) for Sulayman, just as they were subjugated. Yet, they are a subtle creation (خلق رقيق) nourished by breathing (التنسّم). The proof of this is their ascent to the heaven to eavesdrop (لاستراق السمع). A dense body (الجسم الكثيف) cannot ascend to it except by a ladder (سلّم) or a means (سبب)."

In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): Sulayman ibn Dawud lived seven hundred and twelve years.

(١٥) لَقَدْ كانَ لِسَبَإٍ (There was certainly a sign for Saba’) For the descendants of Saba' ibn Yashkhub ibn Ya'rub ibn Qahtan (1). In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He was asked about Saba', whether he was a man or a woman. He replied: "He was a man from the Arabs who fathered ten [tribes]. Six of them went to Yemen (تَيَامَنَ) and four of them went to Sham (تَشَأَّمَ). As for those who went to Yemen, they are الأزد and كندة and مذحج and الأشعرون and الأنمار and حمير. It was asked: 'Who are الأنمار?' He said: 'Those from whom خثعم and بجيلة descend.' As for those who went to Sham, they are عاملة and جذام and لخم and غسّان." فِي مَسْكَنِهِمْ (in their dwelling place) The place of their residence. It is said that it is in Yemen, called مآرِب (Ma'rib), between it and صَنعاء (San'a') is a journey of three [days]. It was also recited in the singular (مَسْكَنِهِمْ), then with the opening of the كاف (مَسْكَنِهِمْ) and its breaking (مَسْكِنِهِمْ). آيَةٌ (a sign) A sign (علامة) indicating the existence of the Chosen Maker (الصانع المختار) and that He is capable of whatever He wills of wondrous matters. Two gardens (جَنَّتانِ): two groups of orchards عَنْ يَمِينٍ وَشِمالٍ (on the right and the left) One group to the right of their town and one group to its left. Each one of them, due to their proximity and closeness, was as if it were a single garden (جنّة واحدة). Thus it was said. كُلُوا مِنْ رِزْقِ رَبِّكُمْ وَاشْكُرُوا لَهُ (‘Eat of the provision of your Lord, and thank Him!) [This is] based on the intention of the saying. بَلْدَةٌ طَيِّبَةٌ وَرَبٌّ غَفُورٌ (A good land and an all-forgiving Lord.’) And the entirety [of the preceding phrase] was also recited in the accusative case (بالنصب).

(١٦) فَأَعْرِضُوا ** (So they turned away) from gratitude فَأَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ سَيْلَ الْعَرِمِ ** (whereupon We unleashed upon them the flood of the dam) meaning, the mighty, severe [flood].

Al-Qummi said: There was a sea in Yemen, and Sulayman (peace be upon him) had commanded his soldiers to draw a channel for them from the fresh sea to the lands of India. They did so and constructed a great dam (عقدة عظيمة) for it, made of rock and lime, so that it would overflow onto their lands. They made outlets (مجاري) for the channel, and whenever they wanted to release water from it, they would release it according to their needs. And they had جَنَّتانِ عَنْ يَمِينٍ وَشِمالٍ ** (two gardens, on the right and the left) [stretching] for a distance of ten days' journey, through which the passerby would walk, and the sun would not fall upon him due to their density (التفافها).

When they committed acts of disobedience and rebelled against the command of their Lord, and the righteous forbade them but they did not desist, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent against that dam the al-juradh (الجرد), which is the large rat. It would uproot the rock that men could not lift and throw it away. When some of the people saw this, they fled and abandoned the lands. The al-juradh (الجرذ) continued to uproot the stones until they destroyed that [dam]. They did not realize [what was happening] until the flood overwhelmed them, ruined their lands, and uprooted their trees. This is His saying, the Almighty: لَقَدْ كانَ لِسَبَإٍ ** (There was certainly a sign for Saba’) the Ayah up to His saying سَيْلَ الْعَرِمِ ** (the flood of the dam) meaning, the mighty, severe [flood]. **وَبَدَّلْناهُمْ بِجَنَّتَيْهِمْ جَنَّتَيْنِ ذَواتَيْ أُكُلٍ خَمْطٍ ** (and We replaced their two gardens with two gardens bearing bitter fruit) [meaning] bitter, repulsive [fruit].

Al-Qummi [said]: And they are Umm Ghaylan (أمّ غيلان). وَأَثْلٍ وَشَيْءٍ مِنْ سِدْرٍ قَلِيلٍ ** (and tamarisk, and a few lote trees) It is said that these are conjoined (معطوفان) to أُكُلٍ ** (fruit) and not to خَمْطٍ ** (bitter fruit), because al-athl (الأثل) is the tamarisk (الطّرفاء) and has no fruit. The lote tree (السّدر) was described as being few (قَلِيلٍ) because its harvest (جناه), which is the nabiq (النّبق), is pleasant to eat, and for that reason, it is planted in orchards. The replacement (البدل) was named "two gardens" (جنّتين) for the sake of correspondence (المشاكلة) and mockery (التهكّم).

(١٧) ذلِكَ جَزَيْناهُمْ بِما كَفَرُوا ** (17) Thus did We requite them for their ingratitude. for their ingratitude for the blessing (النعمة). ** وَهَلْ نُجازِي إِلَّا الْكَفُورَ ** And do We requite anyone but the ungrateful? except the one who is extreme (البليغ) in ingratitude (الكفران). And it was recited (وقرئ) with the nūn (بالنّون) [i.e., نُجازِي] and the kafür (الكفور) in the accusative case (نصب). (١٨) وَجَعَلْنا بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ الْقُرَى الَّتِي بارَكْنا فِيها ** (18) Between them and the towns which We had blessed, We set up conspicuous towns, by granting abundance (التوسعة) to its people. It was said that they are the villages of al-Shām (الشّام), and al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Mecca (مكّة). ** قُرىً ظاهِرَةً ** continuous (متواصلة), where some appear visible to others. ** وَقَدَّرْنا فِيهَا السَّيْرَ ** such that the traveler (الغادي) who sets out in the morning rests at noon (يقيل) in one village and spends the night (يبيت) in another. ** سِيرُوا فِيها ** intending the saying [i.e., meaning We said to them]: ** لَيالِيَ وَأَيَّاماً ** whenever you wished, by night or by day, being secure (آمِنِينَ).

(١٩) فَقالُوا رَبَّنا باعِدْ بَيْنَ أَسْفارِنا ** (19) Then they said, 'Our Lord! Make the stages of our journeys far apart.' They showed ingratitude for the blessing (أَشِرُوا النّعمة) and grew weary of well-being (العافية). So they asked Allah to place deserts (مفاوز) between them and Syria (الشام) so that they might show superiority over the poor by riding mounts (الرواحل) and stocking up provisions (الأزواد). So Allah answered them by destroying the intermediate villages. And it was read as بَعِّد (ba‘‘id).

In al-Majma‘, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [The phrase] رَبَّنا باعَدَ (Our Lord has made distant) is in the form of a statement (بلفظ الخبر), indicating that it was a complaint from them regarding the distance of their journey, due to their excessive indulgence in luxury (الترفيه) and their failure to appreciate what Allah had bestowed upon them. وَظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ (and they wronged themselves) because they rejected the blessing (بطروا النعمة). فَجَعَلْناهُمْ أَحادِيثَ ** (19) So We turned them into legends meaning, people speak of them in astonishment and use them as a proverb, saying: "They scattered like the hands of Saba’ (أيدي سبأ)." وَمَزَّقْناهُمْ كُلَّ مُمَزَّقٍ ** (19) and scattered them into diverse fragments. meaning, We dispersed them with the utmost dispersion, until Ghassan (غسّان) among them joined Syria (الشام), Anmar (أنمار) joined Yathrib (يثرب), Judham (جذام) joined Tihamah (تهامة), and Al-Azd (الأزد) joined Oman (عمّان). إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ ** (19) There are indeed signs in that in what has been mentioned, لَآياتٍ لِكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ ** (19) for every patient [meaning] patient against sins (المعاصي), شَكُورٍ ** (19) and grateful person. [meaning] grateful for the blessings (النعم).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: "These were a people who had continuous villages, where they could see one another, and flowing rivers, and manifest wealth. But they disbelieved in the blessings of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and changed the well-being (عافية) of Allah that was within themselves. So Allah changed the blessing that was with them. إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُغَيِّرُ ما بِقَوْمٍ حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا ما بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (11) Indeed Allah does not change a people’s lot unless they change what is in their souls. So Allah sent upon them the Flood of Al-Arim (سَيْلَ الْعَرِمِ), which drowned their villages, ruined their homes, and took away their wealth. And He replaced their two gardens with جَنَّتَيْنِ ذَواتَيْ أُكُلٍ خَمْطٍ وَأَثْلٍ وَشَيْءٍ مِنْ سِدْرٍ قَلِيلٍ. (16) So We replaced their two gardens with two gardens bearing bitter fruit, tamarisk, and a few lote trees.**

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in the tradition concerning Al-Hasan al-Basri regarding this verse, he (peace be upon him) said: "Rather, Allah has set forth parables (الأمثال) in the Quran concerning us. We are the towns (القرى) which Allah has blessed. That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, concerning those who acknowledge our virtue, where He commanded them to come to us, so He said: وَجَعَلْنا بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ الْقُرَى الَّتِي بارَكْنا فِيها ** (18) and We placed between them and the towns which We had blessed Meaning, We placed between them and their followers (شيعتهم) الْقُرَى الَّتِي بارَكْنا فِيها (the towns which We had blessed) [which are] قُرىً ظاهِرَةً (visible towns). And the visible towns (القرى الظاهرة) are the messengers (الرسل), the transmitters (النقلة) from us to our followers, and the jurists (فقهاء) of our followers. And His saying, glory be to Him: وَقَدَّرْنا فِيهَا السَّيْرَ (and We determined the journey in them). And the journey (السير) is a parable for the knowledge (العلم) that is traveled with. فِيها لَيالِيَ وَأَيَّاماً ** (18) by night and day is a parable for the knowledge that travels during the nights and days from us to them concerning the lawful and the unlawful (الحلال والحرام), the obligatory duties (الفرائض), and the rulings (الأحكام). آمِنِينَ ** (18) in safety, therein, when they take [it] from its source (معدنها) from which they were commanded to take. آمِنِينَ (in safety) from doubt (الشكَ), misguidance (الضلال), and the transition from the unlawful (الحرام) to the lawful (الحلال)."

And narrated from Al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): What is meant by the towns (القرى) are men (الرّجال). Then He recited verses from the Quran concerning this meaning. It was asked: "Who are they?" He said: "We are they." He said: "Have you not heard His saying: سِيرُوا فِيها لَيالِيَ وَأَيَّاماً آمِنِينَ ** (18) 'Travel through them in safety, by night and day.' He said: "Safe from deviation (الزّيغ)."

In al-Ikmal, narrated from Al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: "By Allah, we are الْقُرَى الَّتِي ** (18) and the towns which We had blessed Allah has blessed, and you are the visible towns (القرى الظاهرة)."

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in the tradition of Abu Hanifah, the beginning of which preceded at the end of the Second Introduction (المقدّمة الثانية), regarding سِيرُوا فِيها لَيالِيَ وَأَيَّاماً آمِنِينَ ** (18) 'Travel through them in safety, by night and day.' He said: "With our Qa'im (قائمنا) from the People of the House (أهل البيت) (peace be upon them)."

(٢٠) ** وَلَقَدْ صَدَّقَ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (20) And certainly Iblis found his conjecture to be true concerning them,

إِبْلِيسُ ظَنَّهُ (Iblis) his conjecture. He was truthful in his conjecture (ظنّه), which is His saying: لَأُضِلَّنَّهُمْ ** (I will surely lead them astray) and لَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ ** (I will surely entice them). And it was recited with tashdid (بالتشديد), meaning He confirmed it (حقّقه*). ** فَاتَّبَعُوهُ إِلَّا فَرِيقاً مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (so they followed him, except a group of the faithful).

(21) وَما كانَ لَهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ سُلْطانٍ ** domination and mastery through whispering (وسوسة) and seduction (استغواء) ** إِلَّا لِنَعْلَمَ مَنْ يُؤْمِنُ بِالْآخِرَةِ مِمَّنْ هُوَ مِنْها فِي شَكٍ ** (21) He had no authority over them (except that We might ascertain those who believe in the Hereafter from those who are in doubt about it.) so that the believer may be distinguished from the doubter. By the attainment of knowledge, He intended the attainment of its object (i.e., the manifestation of that knowledge in reality). وَرَبُّكَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ حَفِيظٌ** (and your Lord is guardian over all things.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The interpretation (تأويل) of this Ayah was when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم passed away, and the assumption (الظنّ) was from Iblis when they said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that he speaks out of caprice (الهوى). So Iblis assumed an assumption (ظنّاً) about them, and they confirmed his assumption.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Allah commanded His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to appoint the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) for the people, in His saying: يا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ بَلِّغْ ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** (O Apostle! Communicate what has been sent down to you from your Lord.) concerning Ali at Ghadir Khumm, so he said: "Of whomsoever I am the Mawla (مولاه), Ali is his Mawla (مولاه)." Then the devils (الأبالسة) came to the Greater Iblis and threw dust upon their heads. Iblis said to them: "What is wrong with you?" They said: "This man has today tied a knot that nothing will untie until the Day of Resurrection." Iblis said to them: "Nay! Those around him have promised me a promise concerning him, and they will not break it for me." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down to His Messenger: وَلَقَدْ صَدَّقَ عَلَيْهِمْ إِبْلِيسُ ظَنَّهُ ** (Certainly Iblis found his assumption about them to be true.) the Ayah.

(٢٢) قُلِ للمشركين ادْعُوا الَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ آلهة **مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (22) Say: 'Invoke those whom you claim [to be gods] besides Allah.'

Say to the polytheists: Invoke those whom you claimed to be gods مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (besides Allah) regarding what concerns you, whether it is attracting benefit or repelling harm. لا يَمْلِكُونَ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ ** (They do not control [as much as] the weight of an atom) of good or evil فِي السَّماواتِ وَلا فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (in the heavens or on the earth) concerning the affair of both. وَما لَهُمْ فِيهِما مِنْ شِرْكٍ ** (nor do they have any share in either of them) meaning, any partnership (شركة), neither in creation (خلقاً) nor in ownership (ملكاً). وَما لَهُ مِنْهُمْ مِنْ ظَهِيرٍ (nor is any of them His supporter) who assists Him in managing the affair of both (the heavens and the earth).

(٢٣) وَلا تَنْفَعُ الشَّفاعَةُ عِنْدَهُ ** (Intercession is of no avail with Him) And intercession will not benefit them either, as they claim, ** إِلَّا لِمَنْ أَذِنَ ** (except for him whom He permits) ** لَهُ ** (Him) to intercede. And it was recited (وقرئ) with the dammah on the hamzah (بضمّ الهمزة).

Al-Qummi said: No one among the Prophets of Allah, the friends (أولياء) of Allah, or His Messengers will intercede on the Day of Resurrection until Allah grants him permission, except for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. For Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has already granted him permission for intercession before the Day of Resurrection. Intercession belongs to him and to the Imams عليهم السلام, and then after that, to the Prophets.

And narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: There is no one among the former and the latter generations except that he is in need of the intercession of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله on the Day of Resurrection. Furthermore, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله has the right of intercession for his community (أمّته), and we have the right of intercession for our followers (شيعتنا), and our followers have the right of intercession for their families (أهاليهم). Then He said: Indeed, the believer will intercede for the likes of (مثل) Rabī‘ah and Muḍar. And indeed, the believer will intercede even for his servant (خادمه), saying: "O Lord, by the right of his service to me, he used to protect me from the heat and the cold." ** حَتَّى إِذا فُزِّعَ عَنْ قُلُوبِهِمْ ** (When the fright is removed from their hearts). Meaning: They wait in fear until the fright is removed (كشف) from their hearts. And it was recited (وقرئ) based on the active voice (على البناء للفاعل). ** قالُوا ** (They say). Some of them said to others: ** ما ذا قالَ رَبُّكُمْ قالُوا الْحَقَّ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ ** (What did your Lord say? They say: The truth, and He is the All-exalted, the All-great). [He is] the Possessor of Exaltation (العلوّ) and Grandeur (الكبرياء).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: That is because the inhabitants of the heavens had not heard any revelation (وحياً) between the time ʿĪsā ibn Maryam (peace be upon him) was sent until Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله was sent. So when Allah sent Gabriel (جبرئيل) to Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله, the inhabitants of the heavens heard the sound of the revelation of the Quran like the striking of iron upon a rock (الصفا). So the inhabitants of the heavens were struck unconscious (فصعق). When Gabriel finished the revelation, he descended. Every time he passed by the inhabitants of a heaven, ** فُزِّعَ عَنْ قُلُوبِهِمْ ** (the fright was removed from their hearts). He says: meaning, it was uncovered (كشف) from their hearts. Then some of them said to others: ** ما ذا قالَ رَبُّكُمْ قالُوا الْحَقَّ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ.** (What did your Lord say? They say: The truth, and He is the All-exalted, the All-great.)

footnote (١) The pronoun in ** عَلَيْهِمْ ** refers back to the people of Saba', and it is also said that it refers to all people except those who obeyed Allah.

(٢٤) قُلْ مَنْ يَرْزُقُكُمْ مِنَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (24) Say, ‘Who provides for you from the heavens and the earth?’ This is an affirmation (تقرير) of His saying: لا يَمْلِكُونَ قُلِ اللهُ ** (They do not possess... Say, 'Allah'), since there is no answer other than Him. And in this is an indication that if they remain silent or stammer in the answer for fear of being compelled (الإلزام), then they are affirming it (مقرون به) in their hearts. وَإِنَّا أَوْ إِيَّاكُمْ لَعَلى هُدىً أَوْ فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ ** (24) Indeed, either we or you are rightly guided or in manifest error.’ Meaning, that one of the two parties—the monotheists (الموحّدين) and the polytheists (المشركين)—is certainly upon one of the two matters: guidance (الهدى) or manifest error (الضلال المبين). This is more eloquent than explicit declaration, because it takes the form of fairness (الإنصاف) which silences the contentious opponent (الخصم المشاغب). It is said that the difference between the two particles (الحرفين) [i.e., على (upon) and في (in)] is because the guided person (الهادي) is like one who has ascended a lighthouse (مناراً), observing things and gaining knowledge of them (يطلع عليها), or like one who has mounted a steed (جواداً), riding it wherever he wills. Whereas the misguided person (الضّال) is as if he is immersed in confusing darkness (ظلام مرتبكٍ) where he cannot see, or imprisoned in a dungeon (مطمورة) from which he cannot escape (يتفصّى منها).

(٢٥) قُلْ لا تُسْئَلُونَ عَمَّا أَجْرَمْنا وَلا نُسْئَلُ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ ** (25) Say, 'You will not be questioned about what we have committed, nor will we be questioned about what you do.' This is more conducive to fairness (الإنصاف) and more eloquent in humility (الإخبات), since they attributed the crime (الاجرام) to themselves and the action (العمل) to the addressed (the opponents). (٢٦) قُلْ يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَنا رَبُّنا ** (26) Say, 'Our Lord will bring us together, on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) ** ثُمَّ يَفْتَحُ بَيْنَنا بِالْحَقِ ** then He will judge between us with the truth. He will judge and decide/separate by admitting the truthful ones (المحقّين) into Paradise (الجنّة) and the false ones (المبطلين) into the Fire (النار). وَهُوَ الْفَتَّاحُ (And He is al-Fattāḥ), the Judge (الحاكم), the Decider (الفاصل), الْعَلِيمُ (The All-Knowing) of what ought to be decreed by Him.

(٢٧) قُلْ أَرُونِيَ الَّذِينَ أَلْحَقْتُمْ بِهِ شُرَكاءَ ** (27) Say, ‘Show Me those whom you have associated with Him as partners.’ so that I may see by what attribute you have associated them with Allah in deserving worship. This is an inquiry about their specious argument after the proof has been established against them, as an increase in their refutation. كَلَّا ** (No indeed!) is a deterrent for them from association (of partners) after the invalidation of the comparison. بَلْ هُوَ اللهُ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ ** (Rather, He is Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise), Who is described by dominance (الغلبة) and the perfection of power (القدرة) and wisdom (الحكمة), while these associated ones are characterized by abasement (الذلّة) and are utterly averse to accepting knowledge and power.

**(٢٨) وَما أَرْسَلْناكَ إِلَّا كَافَّةً لِلنَّاسِ ** (28) And We did not send you except to all mankind

except for a general message (رسالة عامّة) for them, derived from the root الكفّ (restraining/encompassing). For when it encompasses them, it has restrained them (كفّتهم) from anyone among them escaping it, بَشِيراً وَنَذِيراً وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ (as a bearer of good news and a warner. But most people do not know,) so their ignorance leads them to oppose you.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, granted Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله the laws (شرايع) of Noah, Abraham, Moses, and Jesus (upon our Prophet and his Household and upon them be peace)." Until he said: "And He sent Him to all (كافّة): to the white and the black, and the Jinn and mankind (الإنس)."

And in Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): Indeed, Abu Talib asked the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "O son of my brother, have you been sent to all mankind (كافّة), or specifically to your people?" He replied: "No, rather I have been sent to all mankind (كافّة): the white and the black, the Arab (العربيّ) and the non-Arab (العجمي) (1). And by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, I shall surely call to this matter the white and the black, those upon the peaks of the mountains and those in the depths of the seas, and I shall surely call the tongues (ألسنة) of Persia (فارس) and Rome (الرّوم)."

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He said to a man who asked Him: "Inform me about the Messenger (الرّسول). Was He general (عامّاً) to mankind? Has not Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said in His decisive Book: وَما أَرْسَلْناكَ إِلَّا كَافَّةً لِلنَّاسِ ** (And We did not send you except to all mankind) – meaning the people of the East and the West, and the people of the heavens and the earth, among the Jinn and mankind (الإنس)? Did He convey His message to all of them?" The man replied: "I do not know." He (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah did not leave Medina, so how could He convey [the message] to the people of the East and the West?" Then He (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, Allah Almighty commanded Gabriel (peace be upon him), so he uprooted the earth with a feather from his wing and set it up for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. Thus, it was before Him like the palm of His hand in His two palms. He would look at the people of the East and the West and address every people in their own languages (ألسنتهم), inviting them to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and to His prophethood, by Himself. Thus, there remained no village (قرية) nor city (مدينة) except that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله invited them by Himself."

(1) This is supported by the Hadith narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah have mercy on him) from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, who said: "I have been granted five things, and I do not say this boastfully: I was sent to the red and the black; the earth was made a purifier (طهورا) and a place of prostration (مسجداً) for me; war booty (المغنم) was made lawful for me, and it was not made lawful for anyone before me; I was granted victory through awe (الرعب), which travels ahead of me for the distance of a month's journey; and I was granted intercession (الشفاعة), which I have reserved for my Ummah on the Day of Resurrection."

(٢٩) وَيَقُولُونَ مَتى هذَا الْوَعْدُ ** (And they say, 'When will this promise be fulfilled,') the one promised by His saying: يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَنا رَبُّناإِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** ('if you are truthful, when will our Lord gather us?') They address this to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the believers.

(٣٠) قُلْ لَكُمْ مِيعادُ يَوْمٍ لا تَسْتَأْخِرُونَ عَنْهُ ساعَةً وَلا تَسْتَقْدِمُونَ ** (Say, 'You have the appointment of a day which you will not put back by a single hour, nor advance it.') when it suddenly overtakes you. And this is a threatening response against their obstinacy (تَعَنُّت) and denial.

(٣١) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ بِهذَا الْقُرْآنِ وَلا بِالَّذِي بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ** (The faithless say, 'We will never believe in this Quran, nor in that which is before it.') nor in the books that preceded it which indicate the Resurrection (البعث). وَلَوْ تَرى إِذِ الظَّالِمُونَ مَوْقُوفُونَ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ ** (If you could see when the wrongdoers are arraigned before their Lord,) in the place of reckoning (المحاسبة), يَرْجِعُ بَعْضُهُمْ إِلى بَعْضٍ الْقَوْلَ (some of them casting the blame on others,) they converse and exchange words. يَقُولُ الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا ** (those who were abased will say) (the followers) لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا ** (to those who were arrogant,) (the leaders): لَوْ لا أَنْتُمْ ** ('Had it not been for you,) had it not been for your leading us astray and hindering us from faith, لَكُنَّا مُؤْمِنِينَ ** (we would surely have been faithful.') by following the Messenger.

** قالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا أَنَحْنُ صَدَدْناكُمْ عَنِ الْهُدى بَعْدَ إِذْ جاءَكُمْ بَلْ كُنْتُمْ مُجْرِمِينَ ** (32) The arrogant ones will say to those who were abased, 'Did we bar you from guidance after it had come to you? Rather, you yourselves were guilty.'

(The arrogant ones) denied that they were barring them from faith, and they established that they themselves were the ones who barred their own souls, as they turned away from the guidance and preferred blind following (التقليد) over it.

(٣٣) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا بَلْ مَكْرُ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ ** (33) And those who were abased will say to those who were arrogant, 'No, rather it was the scheming of night and day, This is a rejection of their rejection (i.e., the rejection made by the arrogant ones). Meaning, our crime was not the barrier (الصّاد); rather, it was your constant scheming against us, night and day, until you corrupted our judgment.

إِذْ تَأْمُرُونَنا أَنْ نَكْفُرَ بِاللهِ وَنَجْعَلَ لَهُ أَنْداداً وَأَسَرُّوا النَّدامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا الْعَذابَ ** when you commanded us to disbelieve in Allah and to set up equals to Him.' And they will conceal their regret when they see the punishment. And both parties concealed regret over the misguidance (الضلالة) and the misleading (الإضلال), and each one hid it from his companion for fear of reproach (التعيير).

Al-Qummi said: They conceal النَّدامَةَ ** (the regret) in the Fire when they see the friend (وليّ) of Allah. It was said: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, what benefit does their concealing of regret afford them while they are in the punishment?" He said: "They dislike the gloating (شماتة) of the enemies."

وَجَعَلْنَا الْأَغْلالَ فِي أَعْناقِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (34) And We will put shackles on the necks of those who disbelieved. Meaning, on their necks. So, He brought the explicit mention (of 'those who disbelieved') to emphasize their condemnation and to indicate the reason (موجب) for their shackling.

هَلْ يُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** Will they be requited except for what they used to do? Meaning, what is done to them is not done except as a recompense (جزاء) for their deeds.

** وَما أَرْسَلْنا فِي قَرْيَةٍ مِنْ نَذِيرٍ إِلَّا قالَ مُتْرَفُوها إِنَّا بِما أُرْسِلْتُمْ بِهِ كافِرُونَ (٣٤) ** (34) We have not sent any warner to any town but its affluent ones have said, 'We indeed disbelieve in what you have been sent with.'

(This is) a consolation for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding what he suffered from his people, and the specification of the affluent ones (المتنعمين) with the denial (التكذيب) is because the great motivator toward arrogance (التكبّر), boasting (المفاخرة) with the adornments (زخارف) of the world, immersion (الانهماك) in desires (الشهوات), and belittling (الاستهانة) those who have not attained them (the adornments). And for that reason, they joined boasting (المفاخرة) and mockery (التهكّم) to the denial (التكذيب).

(٣٥) وَقالُوا نَحْنُ أَكْثَرُ أَمْوالاً وَأَوْلاداً ** (35) And they said, 'We have greater wealth and children,' so we are more deserving of what you call for, if it is possible, وَما نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ ** 'and we will not be punished.' either because the punishment will not occur, or because He honored us with that, so He will not disgrace us with punishment. (٣٦) قُلْ ** (36) Say, in refutation of their assumption, إِنَّ رَبِّي يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ** 'Indeed my Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes and tightens it,' He expands for whomever He wishes and tightens upon whomever He wishes, and that is not due to honor. And that is that وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** 'but most people do not know.' that this is so. In Nahj al-Balagha: "As for the wealthy among the luxurious nations, they showed partisanship (تعصّبوا) for the traces of the places where blessings descended, وَقالُوا نَحْنُ أَكْثَرُ أَمْوالاً وَأَوْلاداً وَما نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ ** 'And they said, "We have greater wealth and children, and we will not be punished." So if partisanship (العصبيّة) is inevitable, then let your partisanship (تعصّبكم) be for noble qualities (مكارم الخصال), praiseworthy deeds (محامد الأفعال), and excellent matters (محاسن الأمور)—those in which glory (المجد) and courage (النّجد) excelled among the houses (بيوتات) of the Arabs and the leaders (يعاسيب) of the tribes, through desirable morals (الأخلاق الرّغيبة), great forbearance (الأحلام العظيمة), magnificent statuses (الاخطار الجليلة), and praiseworthy traces (الآثار المحمودة)."

(٣٧) وَما أَمْوالُكُمْ وَلا أَوْلادُكُمْ بِالَّتِي تُقَرِّبُكُمْ عِنْدَنا زُلْفى ** (37) Neither your possessions nor your children shall bring you any nearer to Us in proximity, proximity (قربة), save him who has faith and acts righteously, by spending his wealth in the way of Allah and teaching his child goodness and righteousness. فَأُولئِكَ لَهُمْ جَزاءُ الضِّعْفِ بِما عَمِلُوا وَهُمْ فِي الْغُرُفاتِ آمِنُونَ ** (37) for such there is a double reward for what they did, and they will be secure in the upper chambers. from distresses (المكاره). And it was recited in the singular (بالتوحيد).

Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: A man mentioned the wealthy and spoke ill of them. So he (peace be upon him) said: "Be silent! For indeed, if the wealthy person maintains ties of kinship (وصولاً برحمه) and is dutiful towards his brethren (بارّاً بإخوانه), Allah will double the reward for him twice, because Allah says: **وَما أَمْوالُكُمْ ** (And neither your possessions...) the Ayah."

And in al-Ilal (العلل): what is close to it (in meaning).

(٣٨) وَالَّذِينَ يَسْعَوْنَ فِي آياتِنا ** (38) And those who strive against Our signs, seeking to frustrate [them]—those will be brought to the punishment. The Almighty's saying: ** وَالَّذِينَ يَسْعَوْنَ فِي آياتِنا ** (And those who strive against Our signs) by rejection (بالردّ) and slander (والطّعن), مُعاجِزِينَ أُولئِكَ فِي الْعَذابِ مُحْضَرُونَ (seeking to frustrate [them]—those will be brought to the punishment). (٣٩) قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ وَيَقْدِرُ لَهُ ** (39) Say, 'Indeed my Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes of His servants, and tightens it for him. And whatever thing you may spend, He will replace it, and He is the best of providers.' The Almighty's saying: ** قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ وَيَقْدِرُ لَهُ ** (Say, 'Indeed my Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes of His servants, and tightens it for him.') This refers to a single person considering two different times, whereas what preceded referred to two different people, so there is no repetition. وَما أَنْفَقْتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَهُوَ يُخْلِفُهُ ** (And whatever thing you may spend, He will replace it) as a compensation (عوضاً), either immediately (عاجلاً) or later (آجلاً). وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ (and He is the best of providers), for indeed, anyone other than Him is merely a means (وسط) in delivering His provision, and their provision-giving (رازقيّته) is not real (لا حقيقة لرازقيّته).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: "Indeed, the Lord, Blessed and Exalted is He, sends down His command every Friday night to the lowest heaven from the beginning of the night, and [He does so] during the last third of every night. And before Him is an angel who calls out: 'Is there any repentant person (تائب) whose repentance will be accepted? Is there any seeker of forgiveness (مستغفر) who will be forgiven? Is there any petitioner (سائل) whose request will be granted? O Allah, grant every spender a replacement (خلفاً) and every withholder destruction (تلفاً).' [This continues] until the dawn breaks. When the dawn breaks, the command of the Lord returns to His Throne (عرشه), and He divides the provisions (الأرزاق) among the servants." Then he (peace be upon him) said: "And this is the saying of Allah: وَما أَنْفَقْتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَهُوَ يُخْلِفُهُ. (And whatever thing you may spend, He will replace it.)"

And in al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Whoever extends his hand with kindness (بالمعروف) when he finds [the means], Allah will replace for him what he spent in his worldly life (دنياه) and multiply it for him in his Hereafter (آخرته)."

And narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): "Whoever believes in the replacement (بالخلف) is generous with the gift (بالعطيّة)." And in another narration: "Whoever is certain of the replacement (بالخلف), his soul is generous with the spending (بالنّفقة)." And it was said to Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "I spend, but I do not see a replacement (خلفاً)." He said: "Do you think that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has broken His promise?" It was said: "No." He said: "Then why is that?" It was said: "I do not know." He said: "If one of you were to earn wealth lawfully (من حلّه), he would not spend a single dirham except that it would be replaced for him."

And narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), who said to one of his freedmen (لمولى له): "Have you spent anything today?" He replied: "No, by Allah." He (peace be upon him) said: "Then from where will Allah replace [provision] for us?"

(٤٠) وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعاً ** (40) And the day He will muster them all The arrogant (المستكبرين) and the oppressed (المستضعفين) ** ثُمَّ يَقُولُ لِلْمَلائِكَةِ أَهؤُلاءِ إِيَّاكُمْ كانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ ** Then He will say to the angels: 'Was it you that these used to worship?' (This is) a reprimand (تقريعا) for the polytheists (لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ), a shaming (تبكيتاً), and a cutting off of their hope (اقناطاً لهم) regarding what they expect from their intercession (شفاعتهم). And the specification of the angels is because they are the noblest (أشرف) of their partners (شركائهم) and the ones among them fit for the address (للخطاب). And it was read with the yā' (بالياء) in it.

** قالُوا سُبْحانَكَ أَنْتَ وَلِيُّنا مِنْ دُونِهِمْ ** (They will say, 'Immaculate are You! You are our guardian, not them.) You are the One Whom we take as a patron instead of them; there is no alliance (موالاة) between us and them. It is as if they clarified by that their disavowal of being pleased with their worship. Then they disregarded that and negated that they (the idolaters) worshipped them (the angels/prophets) in reality (على الحقيقة), by their saying: بَلْ كانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ الْجِنَ ** (Rather, they have been worshipping the jinn.) meaning the devils (الشياطين), since they obeyed them in worshipping other than Allah. أَكْثَرُهُمْ بِهِمْ مُؤْمِنُونَ (Most of them believe in them.)

(٤٢) ** فَالْيَوْمَ لا يَمْلِكُ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ نَفْعاً وَلا ضَرًّا ** (42) So today none of you has any power to benefit or harm another. Since the command (الأمر) therein belongs entirely to Him, because the abode (الدار) is the abode of recompense (جزاء), and He is the sole Recompenser (المجازي) وَنَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ذُوقُوا عَذابَ النَّارِ الَّتِي كُنْتُمْ بِها تُكَذِّبُونَ (42) And We shall say to those who did wrong, 'Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny.'

(٤٣) وَإِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُنا بَيِّناتٍ قالُوا ما هذا ** (43) And when Our manifest signs are recited to them, they say, 'This is nothing but They mean by it the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: except a man who desires to turn you away from what your fathers used to worship, so that he may make you follow him by means of what he considers novel (يستبدعه). وَقالُوا ما هذا ** They mean the Quran إِلَّا إِفْكٌ ** a lie مُفْتَرىً ** fabricated against Allah وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جاءَهُمْ إِنْ هذا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ and the faithless said of the truth when it came to them, 'This is nothing but manifest magic.'

(٤٤) وَما آتَيْناهُمْ مِنْ كُتُبٍ يَدْرُسُونَها calling them to what they are upon وَما أَرْسَلْنا إِلَيْهِمْ قَبْلَكَ مِنْ نَذِيرٍ warning them against abandoning it (the truth). So, from where did this doubt (الشبهة) arise for them? (44) And We did not give them any scriptures that they might study, and We did not send to them any warner before you.

(٤٥) وَكَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ just as they (the current deniers) denied وَما بَلَغُوا مِعْشارَ ما آتَيْناهُمْ It was said: And these (current deniers) did not attain a tenth of what We gave those (previous nations) in strength, longevity, and abundance of wealth; or (alternatively) those (previous nations) did not attain a tenth of what We gave these (current deniers) in clear proofs (البيّنات) and guidance (الهدى). (45) Those who were before them also denied, and they did not attain a tenth of what We had given them.

I say: It is as if, according to both estimations, those (previous nations) were more deserving of denying their messengers than these (current deniers). And upon this (understanding) is carried what al-Qummi narrated marfu'an (with an elevated chain), who said: كَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ their messengers, and what We gave their messengers did not attain a tenth of what We gave Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them). Or it is carried upon the meaning that the virtues (فضائل) of Muhammad and his Family are more deserving of envy (الحسد) and denial, and the giving to Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is a giving to them. Thus, the narration does not contradict the apparent meaning of the Quran. فَكَذَّبُوا رُسُلِي There is no repetition (تكرير) in this, because the first (denial mentioned) is absolute (مطلق) and the second is restricted (مقيّد). فَكَيْفَ كانَ نَكِيرِ Meaning: My censure (إنكاري) of them through destruction (التدمير). So let these (deniers) and whoever is like them beware. (45) So they denied My messengers. So how was My censure!

(٤٦) قُلْ إِنَّما أَعِظُكُمْ بِواحِدَةٍ ** (46) Say, 'I advise you concerning only one thing: I guide you and advise you concerning one characteristic (خصلة واحدة) (1). ** أَنْ تَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ ** that you rise up for Allah, turning away from argumentation (المراء) and blind imitation (التقليد), مَثْنى وَفُرادى (in pairs and singly), separated, two by two, or one by one, for indeed crowding confuses the mind (الخاطر) and mixes up the speech (القول). Then you reflect upon my affair and what I have brought, so that you may know its truth. ** ما بِصاحِبِكُمْ مِنْ جِنَّةٍ ** Your companion has no madness. so that you may know that he has no madness (جنون) that carries him to this. ** إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ لَكُمْ بَيْنَ يَدَيْ عَذابٍ شَدِيدٍ ** He is only a warner to you before a severe punishment.' meaning: before Him (قدّامه).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: ** إِنَّما أَعِظُكُمْ ** (I advise you) concerns the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him). It is the one thing (الواحدة) that Allah mentioned.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith: Indeed, Allah, majestic is His remembrance, sent down the firm resolutions (عزائم) of the Divine Laws (الشرايع) and the verses (آيات) of the obligatory duties (الفرائض) at different times, just as He created the heavens and the earth in six days. Had He willed to create them in less than the blink of an eye, He would have created them, but He made deliberation (الاناة) and management (المدارة) an example for His trustees (أمنائه) and an obligation (ايجاباً) of the proof (الحجّة) upon His creation. The first thing He bound them to was the affirmation of His Oneness (الوحدانيّة) and Lordship (الربوبيّة) and the testimony that there is no god except Allah (لا إله إلّا الله). When they affirmed that, He followed it with the affirmation of prophethood (النبوّة) for His Prophet and the testimony of His messengership (الرسالة) for him. When they submitted to that, He obligated upon them the Prayer (الصلاة), then the Fast (الصوم), then the Pilgrimage (الحجّ), then the Struggle (الجهاد), then the Zakat (الزكاة), then the voluntary charities (الصدقات) and what follows their course from the wealth of al-Fay' (مال الفيء). So the hypocrites (المنافقون) said: "Is there anything else remaining for your Lord upon us after what He has obligated upon us, which He might obligate, so that you mention it, and our souls may be reassured that nothing remains besides it?" So Allah sent down regarding that: ** قُلْ إِنَّما أَعِظُكُمْ بِواحِدَةٍ ** (Say, 'I advise you concerning only one thing:'), meaning the Wilayah (الولاية). Then Allah sent down: إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ (Your guardian is only Allah, His Apostle), [up to] the end of the Ayah.

(٤٧) قُلْ ما سَأَلْتُكُمْ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ** (47) Say: 'Whatever reward I have asked of you for the Message, فَهُوَ لَكُمْ it is for your own sake.' Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse. He said: "That is because the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم asked his people to love (يَوَدُّوا) his relatives and not harm them." As for His saying: it is for your own sake, He says: "Its reward (ثوابه) is for you."

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): Its meaning is that the reward (أجر) for what I have called you to—namely, my response (اجابتي) and its stored benefit (وذخره)—is for you, not for me.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), He says: "The reward (أجر) of the affection (المودّة)—which is the only thing I have asked of you—it is for your own sake. You are guided by it and saved from the punishment of the Day of Resurrection." إِنْ أَجْرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَى اللهِ وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ ** 'My reward lies only with Allah, and He is witness to all things.' He is aware (مطّلع), knowing my truthfulness and the sincerity of my intention (نيّتي).

footnote (١)** And it was said [that it refers] to a single word, which is the word of Monotheism (*كلمة التوحيد*), and it was said [that it refers] to obedience to Allah.

(٤٨) قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِ ** (48) Say, 'Indeed my Lord hurls the truth.' He casts it (يُلْقِيهِ) and sends it down (وَيُنْزِلُهُ) upon whomever He chooses (يَجْتَبِيهِ) from among His servants, the Knower of the Unseen (عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ).

(٤٩) قُلْ جاءَ الْحَقُ ** (49) Say, 'The truth has come.' Islam (الإسلام) ** وَما يُبْدِئُ الْباطِلُ وَما يُعِيدُ ** 'and falsehood neither originates nor restores.' And falsehood (الباطل) has vanished (وَزَهَقَ), meaning polytheism (الشرك), such that no trace (أثر) of it remains.

In al-Amali, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), from his father, from his forefathers (peace be upon them): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم entered Mecca, and around the House (البيت) were three hundred and sixty idols (صنماً). He began to strike them with a stick in his hand, saying: جاءَ الْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ الْباطِلُ إِنَّ الْباطِلَ كانَ زَهُوقاً (The truth has come, and falsehood has vanished. Indeed falsehood is bound to vanish) [Quran 17:81], جاءَ الْحَقُّ وَما يُبْدِئُ الْباطِلُ وَما يُعِيدُ (The truth has come, and falsehood neither originates nor restores) [Quran 34:49].

And in al-Majma', the like of it is narrated from Ibn Mas'ud.

(٥٠) ** قُلْ إِنْ ضَلَلْتُ (50) Say: 'If I go astray, عن الحق فَإِنَّما أَضِلُّ عَلى نَفْسِي ** * from the truth, *then I only go astray to my own detriment; for indeed the evil consequence of my going astray is upon it (my soul). وَإِنِ اهْتَدَيْتُ فَبِما يُوحِي إِلَيَّ رَبِّي إِنَّهُ سَمِيعٌ قَرِيبٌ And if I am guided, it is by virtue of what my Lord reveals to me. Indeed He is all-hearing, near.' He hears every saying and sees every action, even if it is hidden (خفيّاً).

(٥١) وَلَوْ تَرى إِذْ فَزِعُوا If you could see when they are seized with terror! you would see something terrible. فَلا فَوْتَ ** (then there will be no escape) so they will not escape Allah by flight or by a fortress. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: إِذْ فَزِعُوا ** (when they are seized with terror) refers to the sound (الصوت), and that sound is from the heaven. وَأُخِذُوا مِنْ مَكانٍ قَرِيبٍ ** (and they will be seized from a nearby place). He said: They will be swallowed up by the earth from beneath their feet. And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): It is as if I am looking at al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) having rested His back against the Stone (الحجر)... and He continued the narration until He said: When He comes to al-Bayda', the army of al-Sufyani will march out against Him. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will command the earth, and it will seize their feet. And that is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَلَوْ تَرى إِذْ فَزِعُوا فَلا فَوْتَ وَأُخِذُوا مِنْ مَكانٍ قَرِيبٍ.** (If you could see when they are seized with terror! then there will be no escape, and they will be seized from a nearby place.)

(٥٢) ** وَقالُوا آمَنَّا بِهِ ** (And they will say, 'We believe in Him.') He said: meaning by (بِهِ) the Qā’im (القائم) from the Family of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And it was said: meaning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. وَأَنَّى لَهُمُ التَّناوُشُ ** (But how can they attain [faith] from a distant place?) (التَّناوُشُ) means the attainment (تناول) of faith (الإيمان) from a distant place (مِنْ مَكانٍ بَعِيدٍ), meaning after the expiration of the time of obligation (زمان التكليف). He said: Indeed, they sought guidance (الهدى) from where it cannot be attained, while it had been readily offered (مبذولاً) to them from where it could be attained.

(٥٣) ** وَقَدْ كَفَرُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (53) And they had already disbelieved in Him before meaning: the time of accountability (أوان التكليف). وَيَقْذِفُونَ بِالْغَيْبِ ** and cast [slanders] at the Unseen And they stone with conjecture (بالظنّ), and they speak about what has not become manifest to them, from a distant place (مِنْ مَكانٍ بَعِيدٍ), from a remote aspect of His affair (من أمره). (٥٤) ** وَحِيلَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ ما يَشْتَهُونَ ** (54) and a barrier is set between them and what they desire, He said: meaning that they should not be punished. كَما فُعِلَ بِأَشْياعِهِمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** just as was done to their likes before. He said: meaning those who were before them among the deniers perished. إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا فِي شَكٍّ مُرِيبٍ Indeed, they were in a dubious doubt.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sajjad and al-Hasan ibn Ali (peace be upon them both) regarding this Ayah: It refers to the army of al-Bayda' (جيش البيداء); they will be seized from beneath their feet.

And from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): He mentioned a tribulation (فتنة) that will occur between the people of the East (المشرق) and the West (المغرب). He said: While they are thus, the Sufyani (السفياني) will emerge against them from al-Wadi al-Yabis (الوادي اليابس) immediately, until he descends upon Damascus (دمشق). He will then dispatch two armies: one army to the East (المشرق) and another to Medina (المدينة). They will descend upon the land of Babylon (بابل) near the cursed city—meaning Baghdad (بغداد)—where they will kill more than three thousand, disgrace more than one hundred women, and kill three hundred rams (كبش) from Bani al-Abbas (بني العبّاس) there. Then they will descend to Kufa (الكوفة) and destroy what is around it. Then they will depart, heading towards Sham (الشام). A banner of guidance (راية هدى) will emerge from Kufa and catch up with that army, and they will kill them, such that no informant escapes from them, and they will rescue the captives (السبي) and spoils (الغنائم) that were in their hands.

The second army will descend upon Medina (المدينة) and plunder it for three days and nights. Then they will depart, heading towards Mecca (مكّة). When they reach al-Bayda' (بالبيداء), Allah will send Gabriel (جبرئيل) and say: "O Gabriel, go and destroy them (فَابِدْهُمْ)." So he will strike it with his foot, a single strike, and Allah will cause the earth to swallow them (يخسف الله بهم) there. None will escape from them except two men from Juhaynah (جهينة). That is why the saying came: (وعند جهينة الخبر اليقين) (And with Juhaynah is the certain news).

That is His saying, the Almighty: وَلَوْ تَرى إِذْ فَزِعُوا (And if you could see when they are terrified) [Q. 34:51] the Ayah. He said: And our companions (أصحابنا) narrated similar accounts in the traditions concerning the Mahdi (أحاديث المهديّ) from Abu Ja'far and Abu Abdillah (peace be upon them both).

In Thawab al-A'mal (ثواب الأعمال) and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites both al-Hamdayn (الحمدين)—Hamd Saba (Surah Saba) and Hamd Fatir (Surah Fatir)—in a night, he will remain throughout his night in the protection (حفظ) and guardianship (كلاءته) of Allah. He said: And if he recites them during his day, no disliked thing (مكروه) will afflict him during his day, and he will be granted of the good of this world (خير الدنيا) and the good of the Hereafter (خير الآخرة) what has not occurred to his heart and what his desire has not reached.

SURAH AL-MALAIKAH (The Angels)

Meccan. Al-Hasan said: Except for two Ayahs: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَتْلُونَ كِتابَ اللهِ ** (Indeed, those who recite the Book of Allah) the Ayah, and ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا الْكِتابَ ** (Then We made those who We chose from Our servants inherit the Book) the Ayah. Its number of Ayahs is forty-five Ayahs. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful
